files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 1c2dc65..92c27e3 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -131,2 +131,9 @@ Day 126 / Week 18 observations:
 - Tier-2 R&D is still in progress. Competitor quality posts strengthened again, and users are asking for accuracy timelines/benchmarks.
 - Week 18 adjustment: start tier-3 R&D, upgrade capacity to tier 4, keep the proven S2 growth/support settings stable, and post a benchmark/beta-track commitment.
+
+Day 133 / Week 19 observations:
+- Cash finished at $508k after starting tier-3 R&D and moving to tier 4. Weekly operating revenue was $600k against about $322k in operating costs.
+- S2 content is still strong: 1,465 paid content subscribers from 4,094 leads, mostly B/C. No acquisition change needed.
+- The capacity move was necessary: daily usage hit 2.55M, which would have exceeded tier 3. Tier 4 now has ample headroom.
+- Cancellations rose to 120 and downgrades to 183. Social posts point to support lag and quality/benchmark transparency more than price.
+- Week 19 adjustment: hold ads/prices/capacity, increase support and dev spend to protect retention while tier-2 and tier-3 R&D remain in progress, and give a more concrete benchmark-report commitment.
